answers.Are you actively migrating to a new syst: Yes, we are exploring a potential migration
answers.Do you require row-level security (RLS): Yes, RLS is essential for our BI setup
answers.Do you have centralized data governance—: Yes, centralized governance is a must
answers.Do you need to be able to track changes: Somewhat, they would be helpful but aren't critical
answers.How would you describe your team’s data: Advanced: Team members are comfortable working with data, can perform basic analysis, and understand key statistical concepts
answers.Does your team include an Analytics Engi: No, but we have people doing similar tasks
answers.To what extent are BI insights leveraged: Moderately, BI supports decision-making but isn't fully embedded in workflows
answers.How important is the cost associated wit: Moderately important: Cost is considered, but it's balanced with features and other factors
answers.Which of the following monitoring capabi: We have basic monitoring only (can see if systems are up/down)
answers.How much do you rely on writing code or: We mostly use built-in drag-and-drop functionality (10-25% code)
answers.Our BI tooling needs to be the best-in-c: Somewhat important: We occasionally need interactive elements for specific use cases
answers.Our BI tooling needs to be able to share: Very few of our dashboards/reports need external sharing capabilities
answers.Our BI tooling needs to automate deliver: Some of our reports require automated delivery/export
answers.What are some pain points you experience:
Currently on SAP, performance issues
Accessibility, data management
answers.What’s most important in your BI tool se: Ease of use balanced with total cost of ownership
